name: Credential Compromise Response
description: Comprehensive playbook for responding to credential compromise incidents including stolen passwords, account takeover, credential stuffing attacks, and leaked credentials from data breaches.

phases.name: Identification
phases.description: The purpose of the Identification stage is to detect credential compromise, determine scope, and perform immediate containment.

tasks.title: Initiate CIRT Team Meeting and Execute Escalation Procedures
tasks.content: Credential compromise can lead to broader system compromise, data breach, or business disruption. Immediate escalation and response is critical.

subtasks.title: Initiate CIRT meeting
subtasks.content: Initiated emergency meeting of the core CIRT team.

subtasks.title: Notify CIRT members
subtasks.content: Notified all CIRT team members of credential compromise incident.

subtasks.title: Assess severity
subtasks.content: Assessed incident severity based on number of compromised credentials and privilege levels.

subtasks.title: Establish secure communications
subtasks.content: Established secure out-of-band communications in case primary channels are compromised.

subtasks.title: Work with Legal Counsel
subtasks.content: Engaged Legal Counsel for potential data breach and regulatory notification requirements.

tasks.title: Validate Credential Compromise
tasks.content: Confirm that credentials have been compromised and identify the source and method of compromise.

subtasks.title: Review security alerts
subtasks.content: Reviewed security alerts for impossible travel, unusual login times, failed login attempts, or credential stuffing patterns.

subtasks.title: Check dark web monitoring
subtasks.content: Checked dark web monitoring services and breach databases for leaked organizational credentials.

subtasks.title: Analyze authentication logs
subtasks.content: Analyzed authentication logs for suspicious login patterns, geographic anomalies, or credential validation attempts.

subtasks.title: Identify compromise source
subtasks.content: Identified source of compromise: phishing, malware, data breach, credential stuffing, brute force, or insider threat.

subtasks.title: Verify unauthorized access
subtasks.content: Verified whether compromised credentials were used for unauthorized access to systems or data.

tasks.title: Determine Scope of Compromise
tasks.content: Identify all compromised accounts, privilege levels, and systems that may have been accessed.

subtasks.title: Identify compromised accounts
subtasks.content: Identified all user accounts with compromised credentials through log analysis and breach data correlation.

subtasks.title: Assess privilege levels
subtasks.content: Assessed privilege levels of compromised accounts including administrator, privileged user, or standard user accounts.

subtasks.title: Review account activity
subtasks.content: Reviewed activity logs for compromised accounts to identify systems accessed, data viewed, or actions taken.

subtasks.title: Check for lateral movement
subtasks.content: Checked for signs of lateral movement or privilege escalation from compromised accounts.

subtasks.title: Identify password reuse
subtasks.content: Identified users who may have reused compromised passwords across multiple systems or services.

tasks.title: Assess Impact
tasks.content: Assess the operational, data, and regulatory impact of the credential compromise.

subtasks.title: Assess data exposure
subtasks.content: Assessed what sensitive data may have been accessed through compromised credentials including PII, PHI, financial data, or intellectual property.

subtasks.title: Assess business disruption
subtasks.content: Assessed potential for business disruption from locked accounts, system access issues, or attacker actions.

subtasks.title: Assess regulatory impact
subtasks.content: Assessed whether credential compromise triggers data breach notification requirements under applicable regulations.

subtasks.title: Assess reputational damage
subtasks.content: Assessed potential reputational damage from credential compromise and potential data exposure.

tasks.title: Determine Root Cause
tasks.content: Identify how credentials were compromised to inform remediation and prevention.

subtasks.title: Check for phishing
subtasks.content: Checked for phishing campaigns targeting users and credential harvesting attempts.

subtasks.title: Check for malware
subtasks.content: Checked for info-stealer malware, keyloggers, or credential-harvesting trojans on endpoints.

subtasks.title: Review third-party breaches
subtasks.content: Reviewed recent third-party data breaches for leaked organizational credentials from password reuse.

subtasks.title: Check for brute force
subtasks.content: Checked for brute force or password spraying attacks against authentication systems.

subtasks.title: Identify weak passwords
subtasks.content: Identified use of weak, default, or commonly compromised passwords.

tasks.title: Send Communications
tasks.content: Alert affected users and stakeholders about credential compromise.

subtasks.title: Notify affected users
subtasks.content: Notified users with compromised credentials and provided password reset instructions.

subtasks.title: Send security alert
subtasks.content: Sent organization-wide security alert about credential compromise and password security best practices.

subtasks.title: Notify leadership
subtasks.content: Notified executive leadership about scope and severity of credential compromise.

phases.name: Containment
phases.description: The main objective of the Containment stage is to immediately revoke compromised credentials and prevent further unauthorized access.

tasks.title: Disable Compromised Accounts
tasks.content: Immediately disable or lock accounts with compromised credentials to prevent unauthorized access.

subtasks.title: Disable user accounts
subtasks.content: Disabled all user accounts with confirmed compromised credentials in Active Directory and authentication systems.

subtasks.title: Revoke active sessions
subtasks.content: Revoked all active sessions and tokens for compromised accounts across all systems.

subtasks.title: Disable privileged accounts
subtasks.content: Immediately disabled any compromised privileged or administrative accounts.

subtasks.title: Revoke API keys
subtasks.content: Revoked API keys, service account credentials, and application passwords associated with compromised accounts.

tasks.title: Force Password Resets
tasks.content: Force immediate password resets for compromised accounts and potentially affected users.

subtasks.title: Reset compromised passwords
subtasks.content: Forced immediate password reset for all accounts with compromised credentials.

subtasks.title: Reset passwords for password reuse
subtasks.content: Forced password reset for users who may have reused passwords across multiple systems.

subtasks.title: Expire old passwords
subtasks.content: Configured systems to reject old passwords and require new unique passwords.

subtasks.title: Implement temporary passwords
subtasks.content: Generated secure temporary passwords for immediate reset with forced change on first login.

tasks.title: Revoke Authentication Tokens and Sessions
tasks.content: Revoke all authentication tokens, refresh tokens, and persistent sessions that could be used for access.

subtasks.title: Revoke OAuth tokens
subtasks.content: Revoked all OAuth tokens and application authorizations for compromised accounts.

subtasks.title: Clear SSO sessions
subtasks.content: Cleared all single sign-on (SSO) sessions for compromised accounts.

subtasks.title: Invalidate cookies
subtasks.content: Invalidated persistent authentication cookies and remember-me tokens.

subtasks.title: Reset MFA devices
subtasks.content: Reset multi-factor authentication devices and registrations for compromised accounts.

tasks.title: Monitor for Credential Abuse
tasks.content: Implement enhanced monitoring to detect any continued attempts to use compromised credentials.

subtasks.title: Monitor authentication attempts
subtasks.content: Set up alerts for failed authentication attempts using old or compromised credentials.

subtasks.title: Monitor for credential stuffing
subtasks.content: Monitored for credential stuffing attacks attempting to use stolen credentials.

subtasks.title: Track geographic anomalies
subtasks.content: Tracked login attempts from unusual geographic locations or IP addresses.

tasks.title: Block Attacker Infrastructure
tasks.content: Block IP addresses and infrastructure associated with credential compromise attempts.

subtasks.title: Block malicious IPs
subtasks.content: Blocked IP addresses associated with unauthorized login attempts and credential abuse.

subtasks.title: Block suspicious geographies
subtasks.content: Blocked authentication from high-risk geographic regions if not required for business.

subtasks.title: Implement rate limiting
subtasks.content: Implemented rate limiting on authentication endpoints to prevent credential stuffing.

phases.name: Eradication
phases.description: The Eradication stage focuses on removing the root cause of compromise and strengthening credential security.

tasks.title: Remove Malware and Info-Stealers
tasks.content: If credentials were compromised via malware, ensure all malicious software is removed from affected systems.

subtasks.title: Scan for malware
subtasks.content: Performed comprehensive malware scans on all systems used by compromised accounts.

subtasks.title: Remove info-stealers
subtasks.content: Removed credential-stealing malware, keyloggers, and browser password dumpers.

subtasks.title: Rebuild infected systems
subtasks.content: Rebuilt systems with severe infections from clean images.

subtasks.title: Verify clean state
subtasks.content: Verified systems are clean through multiple scanning methods before restoring access.

tasks.title: Implement Multi-Factor Authentication
tasks.content: Deploy or enforce multi-factor authentication across all systems to prevent credential-only compromise.

subtasks.title: Deploy MFA solution
subtasks.content: Deployed or enhanced multi-factor authentication solution across all critical systems.

subtasks.title: Enforce MFA policies
subtasks.content: Enforced mandatory MFA for all users, especially privileged accounts and remote access.

subtasks.title: Disable legacy authentication
subtasks.content: Disabled legacy authentication protocols that don't support MFA (e.g., basic auth, legacy email protocols).

subtasks.title: Implement phishing-resistant MFA
subtasks.content: Prioritized phishing-resistant MFA methods like hardware tokens, FIDO2, or biometrics over SMS.

tasks.title: Strengthen Password Policies
tasks.content: Implement stronger password policies and requirements to prevent weak credential compromise.

subtasks.title: Enforce password complexity
subtasks.content: Enforced strong password complexity requirements including minimum length, character diversity.

subtasks.title: Block compromised passwords
subtasks.content: Implemented password breach database checking to prevent use of known compromised passwords.

subtasks.title: Prevent password reuse
subtasks.content: Configured password history to prevent reuse of recent passwords.

subtasks.title: Implement password manager
subtasks.content: Deployed enterprise password manager to encourage unique strong passwords for each system.

tasks.title: Implement Credential Monitoring
tasks.content: Deploy credential monitoring to detect future compromises early.

subtasks.title: Deploy dark web monitoring
subtasks.content: Deployed dark web monitoring service to detect leaked organizational credentials.

subtasks.title: Monitor breach databases
subtasks.content: Configured monitoring of public breach databases and credential dumps.

subtasks.title: Set up alerts
subtasks.content: Set up real-time alerts when organizational credentials appear in breaches or on dark web.

subtasks.title: Implement SIEM correlation
subtasks.content: Configured SIEM to correlate authentication anomalies with credential compromise intelligence.

phases.name: Recovery
phases.description: The Recovery stage aims to restore user access securely and verify no unauthorized access persists.

tasks.title: Securely Restore User Access
tasks.content: Restore access for legitimate users after verifying identity and securing accounts.

subtasks.title: Verify user identities
subtasks.content: Verified identities of users requesting account restoration through established procedures.

subtasks.title: Re-enable accounts
subtasks.content: Re-enabled user accounts after password reset and MFA enrollment.

subtasks.title: Verify MFA enrollment
subtasks.content: Verified that all restored accounts have properly registered MFA methods.

subtasks.title: Test account access
subtasks.content: Tested that users can successfully authenticate and access required resources.

tasks.title: Audit Account Permissions
tasks.content: Review and right-size account permissions following principle of least privilege.

subtasks.title: Review account permissions
subtasks.content: Reviewed permissions for all affected accounts and removed unnecessary access rights.

subtasks.title: Implement least privilege
subtasks.content: Implemented principle of least privilege for all restored accounts.

subtasks.title: Separate privileged accounts
subtasks.content: Ensured privileged users have separate admin accounts from daily-use accounts.

subtasks.title: Document permission changes
subtasks.content: Documented all permission changes made during recovery for audit trail.

tasks.title: Validate No Unauthorized Access Persists
tasks.content: Verify that no unauthorized access or attacker persistence remains in the environment.

subtasks.title: Review authentication logs
subtasks.content: Reviewed authentication logs for any remaining suspicious activity.

subtasks.title: Check for backdoor accounts
subtasks.content: Checked for creation of backdoor accounts or unauthorized service accounts.

subtasks.title: Verify no data exfiltration
subtasks.content: Verified that no ongoing data exfiltration is occurring from compromised credentials.

subtasks.title: Scan for persistence
subtasks.content: Scanned for persistence mechanisms like scheduled tasks, scripts, or modified configurations.

tasks.title: Issue Final Communications
tasks.content: Communicate incident resolution and new security requirements to users.

subtasks.title: Notify users of resolution
subtasks.content: Notified users that incident is resolved and normal access is restored.

subtasks.title: Communicate security changes
subtasks.content: Communicated new security requirements including MFA, password policies, and monitoring.

subtasks.title: Provide support resources
subtasks.content: Provided IT support resources for users having difficulty with new security requirements.

tasks.title: Resolve Incident
tasks.content: Formally close the credential compromise incident.

subtasks.title: Verify all actions complete
subtasks.content: Verified all remediation actions are complete and environment is secure.

subtasks.title: Change incident status
subtasks.content: Changed incident status to 'resolved'.

phases.name: Lessons Learned
phases.description: The Lessons Learned stage compiles incident information and implements improvements to prevent future credential compromise.

tasks.title: Generate Incident Report
tasks.content: Create comprehensive incident report documenting credential compromise and response.

subtasks.title: Generate report
subtasks.content: Generated incident report using the Reports tab.

subtasks.title: Document compromise source
subtasks.content: Documented how credentials were compromised and attack vectors used.

subtasks.title: Document affected accounts
subtasks.content: Documented number and types of affected accounts, data accessed, and business impact.

subtasks.title: Share with stakeholders
subtasks.content: Shared report with appropriate stakeholders including security, IT, and leadership.

tasks.title: Conduct Lessons Learned Session
tasks.content: Hold session to identify improvements to credential security and incident response.

subtasks.title: Prepare presentation
subtasks.content: Prepared presentation covering incident timeline, root cause, and response effectiveness.

subtasks.title: Host session
subtasks.content: Hosted lessons learned session with Security, IT, and stakeholder teams.

subtasks.title: Identify gaps
subtasks.content: Identified gaps in password policies, MFA coverage, monitoring, and user awareness.

subtasks.title: Create improvement plan
subtasks.content: Created prioritized action plan for implementing security improvements.

tasks.title: Implement Security Awareness Training
tasks.content: Deploy credential security training for all users.

subtasks.title: Deploy password training
subtasks.content: Deployed training on password security, unique passwords, and password manager usage.

subtasks.title: Conduct phishing awareness
subtasks.content: Conducted phishing awareness training to prevent credential harvesting.

subtasks.title: Provide MFA training
subtasks.content: Provided training on MFA setup, usage, and security best practices.

subtasks.title: Conduct simulated attacks
subtasks.content: Planned simulated phishing and credential compromise exercises to test awareness.

tasks.title: Enhance Credential Security Program
tasks.content: Continuously improve credential security based on emerging threats and best practices.

subtasks.title: Expand MFA coverage
subtasks.content: Expanded MFA coverage to all systems and applications.

subtasks.title: Implement passwordless authentication
subtasks.content: Evaluated and implemented passwordless authentication where feasible.

subtasks.title: Enhance monitoring
subtasks.content: Enhanced authentication monitoring and anomaly detection capabilities.

subtasks.title: Update policies
subtasks.content: Updated password and authentication policies based on lessons learned.
